chunks.text: Pablo Picasso was born in 1881 in Malaga, Spain.
He is one of the most influential painter of the twentieth century
leaving behind tens of thousands of works in his lifetime, including sculpture, prints, and ceramics.
Picasso moved to Paris and for a time produced dark - toned painting of what is called his " Blue Period.
" After his " Rose Period," his style would change dramatically again and again.
In 1907, Picasso unveiled Les Demoiselles d'Avignon.
The work shocked the art world with its bold method of breaking the human figure into geometric shape.
It became the starting point of a new art movement that would later be called cubism.
Cubism overturned, from its very ground
the long Western tradition of painting an object from a single perspective.
Picasso and Braque drew shapes seen from many angles at once on a single canvas.
In 1937, in the middle of the Spanish Civil War
the Nazi German air force bombed the small town of Guernica.
Angered by the suffering, Picasso completed the enormous painting Guernica in only a few weeks.
It became a symbol that called the cruelty of war to the attention of the world.
Picasso never settled into a single style.
" It took me a long time to learn to paint like a child," he said in his later years.
Perhaps no one expanded the horizon of contemporary art as widely as he did.
